title: odontites recordonii
intro: Odontites recordonii, also known as Recordon's toothwort, is a species of flowering plant in the family Orobanchaceae. It is a parasitic plant that obtains its nutrients by connecting to the roots of surrounding plants, often heather or grasses. The plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine, and its roots contain flavonoids and phenolic acids, which are believed to have anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, potentially offering relief from respiratory issues and digestive problems. In herbal preparations, the roots of the Odontites recordonii plant can be made into teas or infusions, or used as a tincture, to take advantage of its medicinal properties and health benefits.
